milestone_brief: Wire the Calabi-Yau Hanson family's 27-38 ms generation time directly to a continuous-drag render path — adding a typical_ms: int = 0 field to the Surface dataclass and routing fast surfaces through the debounce's direct-fire channel — so that a researcher dragging any Hanson-family slider sees the surface update at every tick without releasing the control, with a measured round-trip <=80 ms on the dev machine. Slice: surfaces.py (Surface dataclass field + 3 Hanson generators set typical_ms; others default 0); app.py (render-dispatch checks surface.typical_ms > 0 and <=threshold to bypass coarse-LOD direct-fire — coarse-LOD path from e4 must explicitly skip Hanson per AI-6); tests/ (pure-Python dataclass test no Qt). Specialist hints: AI-6 (parametric pipeline — Hanson skips marching cubes + Taubin; coarse-LOD must NOT enable for these); AI-8 (Surface dataclass not frozen — clean field add). Predecessor: e1. Acceptance: CAND-8 live drag <=80 ms; typical_ms field present; off-screen render at non-default Hanson params produces non-empty PolyData (AI-7: cell_normals=True, consistent_normals=False, auto_orient_normals=False).
research_synthesis: Researcher discovered the milestone was already shipped on main 28 commits ago in 42b6c17 (304 lines across 5 files; 10 new tests in tests/test_typical_ms.py): Surface.typical_ms field at surfaces.py:68, FAST_RENDER_THRESHOLD_MS=80 + should_render_on_drag predicate at surfaces.py:79-99, Hanson typical_ms=39/11/18 at surfaces.py:1196/1201/1206, dispatch guard in app.py._on_params_preview_changed:529. All acceptance signals met. User elected to close as already-shipped per Phase-2 gate.
